channel.title: People on Detention Pipeline
channel.description: Recent content in People on Detention Pipeline

item.title: Brian Gootkin — Simultaneously U.S. Marshal AND Montana Corrections Director
item.description: Simultaneously serves as U.S. Marshal and Montana Corrections Director, creating demand for federal detention beds while controlling the state prison supply to fill them.

item.title: Christopher D. Danley — Interior Dept Advisor Actively Trading GEO Group Stock
item.description: Interior Dept advisor who made 237 stock trades in 2 months, buying GEO Group and Palantir stock while the administration expanded detention and surveillance contracts.

item.title: Clark Minor — HHS CIO with $1M-$5M Palantir Holdings, Former Palantir Employee
item.description: HHS CIO who came directly from Palantir holding $1M-$5M in PLTR stock. Controlled HHS tech procurement for his former employer’s customer agency for 5 months before divesting.

item.title: Kash Patel — FBI Director with Palantir Stock and Goldman Sachs Holdings
item.description: FBI Director holding $50K-$100K in Palantir stock while leading an agency that shares data with ICE through Palantir-built infrastructure. Also runs a $500K-$1M security consulting firm.

item.title: Kevin R. Rhodes — Federal Procurement Policy Administrator with Palantir Stock
item.description: Federal Procurement Policy Administrator holding Palantir stock while setting government-wide acquisition rules that affect every Palantir contract across the entire executive branch.

item.title: Rodney S. Scott — CBP Commissioner Who Owns Border Security Consulting Firm
item.description: CBP Commissioner who owns Honor Consulting Plus LLC, a border security consulting firm. Controls CBP procurement for the same industry his firm consults for.

item.title: Stephen Miller — Homeland Security Advisor with Palantir and Amazon Holdings
item.description: White House Homeland Security Advisor and architect of mass deportation policy who held $100K-$250K in Palantir stock for 7 months while driving demand for its ICE contracts.

item.title: Tae D. Johnson — Former ICE Acting Director, Now Sabot Consulting
item.description: Former Acting ICE Director (31 years ICE/INS) now at Sabot Consulting, advising counties on building IGSA detention facilities. GAO found his service violated the Federal Vacancies Reform Act.

item.title: Thomas D. Homan — Border Czar, Highest Immigration Enforcement Authority
item.description: Border Czar with 312 financial holdings including JPMorgan high-yield bonds and Amazon stock. Sets enforcement priorities that drive detention demand benefiting companies in his portfolio.

item.title: Troy Dean Edgar — DHS Deputy Secretary with Palantir, Defense Contractor Holdings
item.description: DHS Deputy Secretary (#2 at DHS) who held Palantir, Raytheon, and Amazon stock before bulk divestiture. Retains $250K-$500K in IBM stock while overseeing ICE and CBP.

item.title: William K. Marshall III — Bureau of Prisons Director
item.description: Bureau of Prisons Director overseeing 122 federal institutions and private prison contracts with GEO and CoreCivic. No personal financial conflicts but controls key detention infrastructure.

item.title: Antoine McCord — DHS CIO, Former Anduril Employee with Active Equity
item.description: DHS CIO Antoine McCord is a former Anduril employee holding active Anduril equity. Paired with ex-Palantir OMB CIO Barbaccia, both Thiel-network alumni control federal tech procurement.

item.title: David Venturella
item.description: Spent 12 years at GEO Group ($6M+ pay), now runs ICE detention contracting with an ethics waiver. Since his return, GEO won $520M in new contracts and reported 700% profit increase.

item.title: Edward D'Alelio — Blue Owl Capital Director, Former Trump Entertainment Resorts Board
item.description: Blue Owl Capital director who previously served on the Trump Entertainment Resorts board, connecting the private equity firm profiting from ICE warehouses to the Trump business network.

item.title: Edward Forst — GSA Administrator, Former Goldman Sachs AND Deutsche Bank Employee
item.description: GSA Administrator and former Goldman Sachs partner whose agency’s procurement role was bypassed via WEXMAC-TITUS. All $1.074B in warehouse purchases closed after his Dec 2025 swearing-in.

item.title: John Russell McGranahan — Former GSA General Counsel with Blue Owl Investments
item.description: Former GSA General Counsel (through Nov 2025) who held Blue Owl Capital investments while at the agency that normally brokers federal real estate. Blue Owl sold Tremont PA for $119M.

item.title: Daniel A. Bible
item.description: Former ICE ERO deputy director who left days before the 2024 election to become GEO Group EVP. GEO invested $70M in detention expansion weeks after his hire, suggesting advance knowledge.

item.title: Daniel H. Ragsdale
item.description: Former ICE Deputy Director (2012-2017) who joined GEO Group as EVP two months after leaving government. Now SVP for Contract Administration and Compliance at GEO.

item.title: Matthew T. Albence
item.description: Former Acting ICE Director (2019-2020) who joined GEO Group as SVP Client Relations. Known for calling family detention centers ‘more like summer camp.’ One of 6+ ex-ICE officials at GEO.
